entities.name: PULSES
entities.description: Leguminous crops processed into dhal for human consumption

entities.name: DEHULLING
entities.description: The process of removing the outer hull from pulses

entities.name: DHAL
entities.description: Decorticated dry split cotyledons of pulses

entities.name: COOKING TIME
entities.description: The duration required to cook pulses, which is reduced by dehulling

entities.name: ANTINUTRITIONAL FACTORS
entities.description: Compounds in pulses that interfere with nutrient absorption, reduced by dehulling

entities.name: PROTEIN QUALITY
entities.description: The nutritional value of protein in pulses, improved by dehulling

entities.name: PALATABILITY
entities.description: The taste and texture of pulses, improved by dehulling

entities.name: DIGESTIBILITY
entities.description: The ease with which pulses are digested, improved by dehulling

entities.name: DEHULLING LOSSES
entities.description: The amount of material lost during the dehulling process

entities.name: DHAL YIELD
entities.description: The quantity of dhal produced from pulses

entities.name: DEHULLING EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The effectiveness of the dehulling process in producing dhal

entities.name: CHICKPEAS
entities.description: A pulse crop characterized by high dhal yield

entities.name: PIGEONPEAS
entities.description: A pulse crop characterized by low dhal yield

entities.name: HEATING
entities.description: A pretreatment process applied to seeds before dehulling to improve yield

entities.name: SEED SHAPE
entities.description: The physical form of seeds, where uniform and round seeds are preferred for higher dhal yields

entities.name: GENOTYPIC DIFFERENCES
entities.description: Genetic variations that influence the dehulling characteristics of pulses

entities.name: NUTRIENT LOSSES
entities.description: The loss of nutrients occurring during the dehulling process

relationships.description: PULSES are processed using the DEHULLING method
DHAL YIELD is a function of DEHULLING EFFICIENCY
STONE CHAKKI operations result in higher DEHULLING LOSSES
DHAL MILL operations result in lower DEHULLING LOSSES
HEATING as a pretreatment improves DHAL YIELD
HEATING is particularly effective for improving yield in PIGEONPEAS
Uniform and round SEED SHAPE leads to higher DHAL YIELD
GENOTYPIC DIFFERENCES affect the characteristics of DEHULLING
